features.description: Per-crate manifests and the generated dependency lockfile required by downstream features.
Provider-scoped persisted Fast service-tier controls and slash-command integration.
Provider-scoped ChatGPT Codex OAuth, credential storage, refresh, usage, and logout integration.
Provider-aware model discovery, sampling, usage accounting, and session routing for Codex.
Codex-authenticated read, search, fetch, image, and provider-key tool adapters.
Read-only manifest, immutable-history, candidate-coverage, launcher, and regression-test control plane.
Credential-free CI checks for provider isolation, Warp provenance, Enhanced identity, updater routing, generated workspace discipline, ownership, and secret-file hygiene.
Credential-free normalized standalone-search contract snapshot, pinned-source extractor, drift classification, and aggregate fork validation.
Fork-owned native macOS/Linux GitHub Release packaging, provenance, curl installation, ownership checks, and local contract tests.
Fork-owned GitHub Releases discovery, exact native asset installation, Homebrew ownership, and no-fallback update routing.
Implemented experimental OpenAI Codex subscription provider contract reference.
Provider-scoped Kimi Code API-key storage, login, catalog discovery, usage lookup, request authentication, and logout integration.
First-class Kimi Code provider identity, credential-bound dynamic models, catalog-selected inference protocols, reasoning controls, cache affinity, and isolated session routing.
Enabled-by-default Kimi hosted search and SSRF-screened hosted fetch with explicit authentication and quota errors plus safe local fallback.
Provider-bound Kimi plan usage retrieval, drift-tolerant quota and Extra Usage parsing, interactive usage summaries, and provider-console routing.
Implemented experimental Kimi Code provider contract, capability boundaries, security policy, and source-reviewed behavior.
Research and implementation design for a possible, currently unimplemented Z.AI Coding Plan provider.
Provider-neutral downstream compatibility changes in pager and render behavior.
Provider-neutral downstream compatibility changes in shell session and auth orchestration.
Downstream compatibility changes in shared agent, chat-state, and memory behavior.
Provider-neutral downstream compatibility changes in the generic tool runtime.
Downstream compatibility changes in environment, workspace, and permission handling.
Fork identity, upstream ledger, repository rules, and source-attribution updates.
Grok Build Enhanced identity across documentation, version output, startup, full-screen, minimal, and upstream-content labels.
Terminal-native, resolved palette, ANSI syntax, and system-appearance behavior.
Downstream packaging metadata, user documentation, and legal attribution surfaces.
Minimal credential-header and 401-attribution boundaries that never serialize credential bytes.
Packaged Warp theme catalog, translation, selection, watching, and attribution.
